description_long: This triumphal arch features a semi-circular arrangement of multiple stone columns supporting a series of terracotta-colored arches. The columns are evenly spaced and rest on square bases, with simple capitals that evoke classical architectural motifs. The arches themselves are smooth and unadorned, forming a continuous arcade with a rhythmic repetition. The structure is open, allowing passage through the archways, and lacks any visible doors or barriers. There are no decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels present, giving the object a clean and utilitarian appearance. The overall design suggests inspiration from Roman or early medieval cloister architecture, with a focus on symmetry and structural clarity.
description: A semi-circular arcade of stone columns supporting terracotta arches, open and unadorned, evoking classical Roman architectural style.
description_view_0: This view highlights the curvature of the arcade and the alignment of the columns, showing the open passageways between them.
description_view_1: From this angle, the full length of the arch sequence is visible, emphasizing the repetitive arches and the even spacing of the columns.
description_view_2: This perspective shows the side of the structure, revealing the depth of the columns and the thickness of the arches.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the back of the columns and arches, with the curvature and open design clearly visible.
